audit_items.proof_trace: The only pointer dereference occurring in thr_posix.c source code itself is `*data = pthread_getspecific(key)` at line 360 (all other pointer params are forwarded directly to libc pthread functions). No NULL check guards `data` before this dereference; the function is exported as a public API symbol (ldap.map:345), so callers can pass NULL. The sole internal call site (tpool.c:1457) passes `&ctx` (stack address, always non-NULL), so no current code path triggers the defect.
audit_items.attack_scenario: A caller invoking `ldap_pvt_thread_key_getdata(key, NULL)` — whether an external plugin or a misbehaving library consumer — would cause a NULL dereference crash (SIGSEGV); no LDAP-protocol-reachable path to pass NULL as the `data` argument has been identified in the current codebase.
